title: Alfuzosin: MedlinePlus Drug Information

What special precautions should I follow? 
Before taking alfuzosin, tell your doctor or pharmacist if you are allergic to this medication, any part of this medication, or any other medications, foods or substances. Tell your doctor or pharmacist about the allergy and what symptoms you had. tell your doctor if you have liver disease. Your doctor may tell you not to take alfuzosin. some medications should not be taken with alfuzosin. Other medications may require dosing changes or extra monitoring when taken with alfuzosin. Make sure you have discussed any medications you are currently taking or plan to take before starting alfuzosin with your doctor and pharmacist. Before starting, stopping, or changing any medications while taking alfuzosin, please get the advice of your doctor or pharmacist. the following nonprescription product may interact with alfuzosin: cimetidine. Be sure to let your doctor and pharmacist know that you are taking these medications before you start taking alfuzosin. Do not start any of these medications while taking alfuzosin without discussing with your healthcare provider. tell your doctor if you or any member of your family have an irregular heartbeat; or if you have or have ever had prostate cancer; angina (chest pain); low blood pressure; or heart or kidney disease; and i
